source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/03_A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/12_Biocompatibility/A-TCF-5-144 Biocompatibility Assessment Report.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Biocompatibility assessment report for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to select biocompatibility tests and assess biological safety for the listed digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
keyPoints: The device is categorized for skin surface contact with limited exposure of 24 hours or less.
Selected biological evaluation tests are cytotoxicity, sensitization, and irritation or intracutaneous reactivity.
Cytotoxicity, irritation or intracutaneous reactivity, and sensitization test results were reported as satisfied.
The final assessment states the products are suitable to use as medical devices and biologically safe based on the results.

entities.name: FDA
entities.evidence: Selection of biological evaluation test according to FDA biocompatibility guideline

concepts.name: Biocompatibility assessment
concepts.evidence: The purpose of this assessment is to ensure that no undue risks arise from biological hazards associated with materials of manufacture and final devices.

concepts.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
concepts.evidence: Common name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

concepts.name: Skin surface contact
concepts.evidence: Nature of body contact: Skin surface

concepts.name: Limited exposure
concepts.evidence: Duration of contact: Limited exposure (≤ 24 Hours)

concepts.name: Irritation or intracutaneous reactivity
concepts.pageTarget: ISO 10993 Irritation and Intracutaneous Reactivity Testing

concepts.evidence: ISO 10993-10: Test for Irritation and Sensitization

evidence.claim: The document number is A-TCF-5-144 and revision is Rev.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Technical Documentation Document No. A-TCF-5-144 Rev. No. 0; Report No.: A-TCF-5-144 (Rev.0)
Biocompatibility Assessment Report Rev. Date Nov/10/2022; Prepared R&D team Kim, Kihyun Nov/10/2022

evidence.claim: The report covers A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW digital flat panel X-ray detector models.
evidence.evidenceText: Biocompatibility Assessment Report for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector Models A1417MCW A1717MCW F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The listed manufacturer is H&abyz Co., Ltd.
The device is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise diagnoses for physicians and radiologists.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film-based radiographic diagnostic systems and provide more precise case diagnoses for physicians and radiologists.

evidence.claim: The contact part is the deco sheet.
The device contact category is skin surface with limited exposure of 24 hours or less.
evidence.evidenceText: Nature of body contact: Skin surface; Duration of contact: Limited exposure (≤ 24 Hours)

evidence.claim: The report selects cytotoxicity, sensitization, and irritation or intracutaneous reactivity tests according to the FDA biocompatibility guideline and ISO 10993 Part 1.
evidence.evidenceText: Selection of biological evaluation test according to FDA biocompatibility guideline (Use of International Standard ISO-10993, ‘Biological Evaluation of Medical Devices - Part 1: Evaluation and Testing); Tests selection Cytotoxicity O; Sensitization (Delayed-type hypersensitivity) O; Irritation or intracutaneous reactivity O

evidence.claim: Applied standards include ISO 10993-1, ISO 10993-5, ISO 10993-10, and ISO 10993-12.
evidence.evidenceText: Applied Standards - ISO 10993-1: Selection of Tests - ISO 10993-5: Test for Cytotoxicity - ISO 10993-10: Test for Irritation and Sensitization - ISO 10993-12: Sample Preparation
evidence.pageTarget: ISO 10993 Biological Evaluation of Medical Devices

evidence.claim: Cytotoxicity testing to ISO 10993-5:2009 by Korea Testing Laboratory report 20-0094 was satisfied.
evidence.evidenceText: Cytotoxicity ISO 10993-5:2009 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0094 Satisfied

evidence.claim: Irritation or intracutaneous reactivity testing to ISO 10993-10:2010 by Korea Testing Laboratory report 20-0095 was satisfied.
evidence.evidenceText: Irritation or Intracutaneous Reactivity ISO 10993-10:2010 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0095 Satisfied

evidence.claim: Sensitization testing to ISO 10993-10:2010 by Korea Testing Laboratory report 20-0096 was satisfied.
evidence.evidenceText: Sensitization ISO 10993-10:2010 Korea Testing Laboratory (KTL) 20-0096 Satisfied

evidence.claim: The final assessment concludes that A1417MCW, A1717MCW, and F1417MCW are suitable to use as medical devices and biologically safe based on the results.
evidence.evidenceText: In conclusion, A1417MCW/A1717MCW/F1417MCW is suitable to use as medical device and biological safety based on the above results.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is based on the document's reference to FDA biocompatibility guideline; the source itself is manufacturer technical documentation, not an FDA-issued record.
Some table text appears OCR-degraded or poorly structured, including endpoint table formatting and misspellings such as 'Irrtation' and 'Degradaton'.
